task.name: Blind 8-dimension plan audit
task.objective: Read the ACTIVE GOAL's plan artifacts (goal.md, specs, tasks.yaml), evaluate against an 8-dimension checklist with a severity-weighted scoring rubric, and report the verdict back to the planner via windows-message.

glossary.term: Name of this auditor's tmux window — always goal-namespaced: plan-audit-{ns} where {ns} is the goal namespace (e.g. plan-audit-002 for goal-002). The planner (plan.xml) derives the name and creates the window; the auditor reads its own window name from context. In standalone mode (no active goal), falls back to bare "plan-audit".
Name of the planner window that spawned this audit. Extracted from the task message or inferred as the supervisor window for the active goal.

The active goal being audited. Resolved from: (1) leading token in $ARGUMENTS if it matches goal-\d+, (2) .tmux-cli/taskvisor-current-goal marker, (3) goals.yaml current_goal field. The audit is SCOPED to this single goal.
.tmux-cli/goals/{GOAL_ID}/ — the goal's directory containing goal.md, tasks.yaml, research/.
{GOAL_DIR}/research/ — where specs and context files live.
Current audit pass number (1-based). Derived from existing plan-audit-*.md files in RESEARCH_DIR.

scoring-rubric.note: Start at 100 points. Deduct per finding by severity. A plan passes when score >=90 AND zero open SEV-1 or SEV-2 findings remain.
scoring-rubric.severity: Critical: blocks execution outright or produces wrong results (missing validate, circular dependency, impossible acceptance criterion).
Major: likely to cause retry loops, cascading failures, or silent data corruption (ambiguous validate, tautological acceptance, missing depends_on edge).
Moderate: unclear spec that may confuse the executor or waste retries (vague scope, overlapping deliverables, under-specified investigation config).
Minor: style or convention violation that does not block execution (description over 120 chars, missing fan-out hints, non-standard phase name).

llm.mandate: SCOPE TO ACTIVE GOAL ONLY — never audit other goals in goals.yaml, only GOAL_ID
NEVER modify any project files, goals, specs, or code — the auditor is strictly read-only
NEVER weaken validate commands or acceptance criteria to improve the score — reach 90% by fixing the plan, never by weakening validate/acceptance
NEVER use AskUserQuestion or any interactive prompt — all decisions are autonomous
Write verdict files ONLY under GOAL_DIR/research/ (never docs/architecture/)
ALWAYS report verdict back to PLANNER_WID via windows-message with [AUDIT:PASS] or [AUDIT:FAIL file=path]
One pass per invocation — the 3-pass stop condition is driven by the CALLER re-invoking plan-audit

step.title: Resolve goal context
step.action: Parse $ARGUMENTS for a leading goal-\d+ token. If found, set GOAL_ID from it.
If no argument, read .tmux-cli/taskvisor-current-goal. If present, set GOAL_ID from it.
If still unresolved, read goals.yaml current_goal field.

check.action: Print: "ERROR: No active goal found — nothing to audit."

step.action: Derive PASS_N from existing {RESEARCH_DIR}/plan-audit-*.md files (max N + 1, or 1 if none).
Resolve PLANNER_WID: read {GOAL_DIR}/supervisor-window if it exists (trim whitespace), else use "supervisor".

step.title: MCP precondition and project context
step.action: Call windows-list to verify tmux-cli MCP is available.
Read AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md for project conventions.

step.title: Read goal artifacts (blind, scoped to GOAL_ID)
step.action: Read all spec files in {RESEARCH_DIR}/ — the implementation specs.
Read the GOAL_ID entry from goals.yaml — validate rules, acceptance, scope, depends_on.
Read any prior audit files: {RESEARCH_DIR}/plan-audit-*.md for re-verification.
step.rule: This is a BLIND audit — never read planner conversation history, only on-disk artifacts.
SCOPE: audit ONLY this goal's artifacts. Do NOT read or evaluate other goals.

substep.title: Command validity
substep.action: Check that each validate command is a concrete, runnable shell command.

substep.title: Dependency order
substep.action: Verify depends_on edges referencing this goal are valid (no cycles, no missing refs).

substep.title: CMD-CONV wrapping
substep.action: Verify each validate command is a bare command (no shell wrappers, no chains).

step.title: 8-dimension checklist (scoped to GOAL_ID)
step.action: Evaluate GOAL_ID against all 8 dimensions. Record findings with severity, dimension, file:line, description.

dimension.name: validate-executability
dimension.description: Every validate command must be a concrete, project-runnable shell command.

dimension.name: dependency-correctness
dimension.description: Declared depends_on edges match the goal graph: no dangling references, no missing edges.

dimension.name: runtime-state-gating
dimension.description: Goals requiring runtime state must depend on the goal that provisions it.

dimension.name: host-container-split
dimension.description: Validate commands must target the correct execution context.

dimension.name: objective-acceptance
dimension.description: Acceptance criteria must be objectively decidable. No subjective language.

dimension.name: spec-discovery-consistency
dimension.description: Goal specs must be consistent with the goal's own acceptance and validation rules.

dimension.name: scope-sanity
dimension.description: Goal scope must be well-bounded and reference real paths.

step.title: Score calculation
step.action: Start at 100, deduct by severity. Floor at 0.

step.title: Re-verify RESOLVED claims
step.action: If prior audit files exist, re-verify each RESOLVED claim against current state.

step.title: Verdict and report

branch.condition: score >= 90 AND zero SEV-1 AND zero SEV-2
branch.action: Send verdict to planner via windows-message to PLANNER_WID:
Write {RESEARCH_DIR}/plan-audit-{PASS_N}.md with score, findings table, remediation priority.
Send verdict to planner via windows-message to PLANNER_WID:

step.rule: The windows-message is MANDATORY — the planner waits for it. Without it, the planner hangs until timeout.

execution-rules.rule: Scoped to GOAL_ID only — never audit other goals
Report back via windows-message with [AUDIT:PASS ...] or [AUDIT:FAIL ...] — MANDATORY
Role separation: auditor never edits goals, specs, code
Scoring: 100 baseline; SEV-1=−25, SEV-2=−15, SEV-3=−8, SEV-4=−3; floor at 0
Approval threshold: score >= 90 AND zero open SEV-1/SEV-2
